Title: Unraveling mannan biosynthesis in poplar and spruce: functional characterization of Cellulose Synthase-Like family A (CSLA) genes

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Populus tremula × Populus alba
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: mannan biosynthesis; hemicellulose biosynthesis in secondary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: secondary cell wall glucomannan backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes the β-(1,4)-linked glucomannan/mannan backbone in the Golgi, producing non-patterned glucomannan lacking galactosyl side chains in the tested system
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose Synthase-Like family A glycosyltransferase with conserved DDDQxxRW motif and multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in poplar xylem; most highly expressed putative CSLA in wild-type trees; significantly downregulated in GolS transgenic trees with reduced mannan
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transgene expressed in Arabidopsis stems under AtCESA7 promoter; in poplar native gene functions in xylem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: glasshouse growth; saccharification tested with and without dilute-acid pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: restores or enables mannan biosynthesis and is the primary determinant of poplar xylem mannan accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCESA7::PtaCSLA1 restored mannose in Arabidopsis csla2,3,9 stems to near wild-type levels. 2. PACE indicated PtaCSLA1 synthesizes non-patterned glucomannan lacking galactosyl side chains. 3. CRISPR mutation of PtaCSLA1 alone abolished detectable mannan in poplar xylem, indicating it is the main contributor to stem mannan biosynthesis. 4. Loss of PtaCSLA1 caused little effect on growth and only marginal effects on saccharification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis mutant complementation; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in poplar; cell wall monosaccharide profiling by HPAEC; PACE analysis; immunolabeling with LM21 antibody
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major/primary contributor to mannan biosynthesis in poplar xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis stems; poplar xylem secondary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall structural hemicellulose affecting secondary cell wall architecture and biomass processing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no substantial alteration in poplar height, stem diameter, fresh biomass, or major wall sugar composition; saccharification effects only marginal
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single PtaCSLA1 knockout and PtaCSLA1/2 double knockouts were indistinguishable in mannan loss, implying PtaCSLA2 contribution in xylem is negligible under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target to eliminate stem mannan in poplar without growth penalty; valuable for engineering cell wall composition and testing roles of mannan in wood properties and processing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression database from GolS transcriptome (Unda et al., 2017); no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Picea engelmannii × Picea glauca × Picea sitchensis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Populus tremula × Populus alba
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: mannan biosynthesis; hemicellulose biosynthesis in secondary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: secondary cell wall galactoglucomannan/glucomannan backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes the β-(1,4)-linked glucomannan/mannan backbone; when expressed in Arabidopsis it produces non-patterned glucomannan with 4-Man linkages and no detectable galactosyl side chains
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose Synthase-Like family A glycosyltransferase with conserved DDDQxxRW motif and multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: in poplar overexpression lines, transgene expressed in developing xylem with line-to-line variation; highest in line 3
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: glasshouse growth; saccharification tested with and without dilute-acid pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: restores mannan biosynthesis in Arabidopsis csla2,3,9 and increases poplar stem mannan upon ectopic expression without growth penalty
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCESA7::PgCSLA1 complemented Arabidopsis csla2,3,9, restoring mannose to wild-type or above wild-type levels. 2. Linkage analysis detected 4-Man only in wild type and PgCSLA1-complemented lines, confirming mannan production. 3. PACE showed PgCSLA1 produces non-patterned glucomannan lacking galactosyl side chains in Arabidopsis. 4. Ectopic expression in poplar increased stem mannose by up to 11.6% in top lines without measurable growth penalty. 5. Overexpressing lines showed slight improvement in saccharification, strongest in the highest-expressing line.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis mutant complementation; linkage analysis by GC-MS; PACE analysis; ectopic overexpression in poplar; RT-qPCR; cell wall monosaccharide profiling by HPAEC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as rate-limiting; sufficient to increase mannan when ectopically expressed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis stems; poplar xylem secondary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall structural hemicellulose affecting secondary wall properties and biomass processing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no significant differences in poplar stem diameter, height, biomass, or lignin; only minor shifts in some wall sugars in certain lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although proposed as candidate for spruce galactoglucomannan backbone synthesis, in Arabidopsis the product lacked galactosyl side chains, indicating additional factors beyond CSLA determine final mannan structure
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising transgene to modestly increase wood mannan content in trees without yield penalty for industrial mannan production or cell wall engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR for transgene expression; no public accession reported
